NOTIFICATIONS BY GOVERNMENT 
AGRICULTURE DEPARTMENT
Secretariat, 10th March 2021.
Notiﬁ cation of Agricultural and Horticultural Crops under Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana 
during Rabi Season 2020-2021
[AGRICULTURE – NOTIFICATION OF AGRICULTURAL AND HORTICULTURAL CROPS, DISTRICTS, INSURANCE 
UNITS AND ACTURIAL PREMIUM RATE FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF REVAMPED PRADHAN MANTRI FASAL BIMA 
YOJANA (RPMFBY) DURING RABI SEASON, 2020-2021 – PUBLICATION IN THE TAMIL NADU GOVERNMENT 
GAZETTE – ORDERS – ISSUED.]

5.
Minutes of 48th State Level Coordination Committee on Crop Insurance (SLCCCI) meeting held on 
12.11.2020 for notiﬁ cation of Crops and Insurance Units under Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal 
Bima Yojana (RPMFBY) during Rabi Season 2020-2021.
6.
From the Director of Agriculture Letter No.CIS1/1008034/2020, dated 16.11.2020.
7.
Government D.O.letter No.21140/AP2/2020-1, Agriculture (AP2) Department, dated 17.11.2020.
*****
ORDER: No. 276, Agriculture (AP2), 27th November 2020.
The Government of India have communicated the policy changes and requested to implement Revamped 
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana from Kharif 2020 onwards. However, in response to the request made by the 
State Government, Government of India have also permitted Government of Tamil Nadu to implement Revamped 


2
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana adopting existing premium rates for Kharif 2020 alone and negotiate with the 
existing Insurance Companies or invite fresh bid from Rabi 2020-2021 onwards.
2. 
In Government Order ﬁ rst read above, the Government have accorded administrative approval for 
implementation of Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana in all the districts of Tamil Nadu except Chennai 
from Kharif 2020 season as per the existing Government of India guidelines (revised).
3. 
In Government Order second read above, the Government have directed to implement the Revamped 
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana in all the districts of Tamil Nadu except Chennai during Special season 
(Samba) and Rabi season 2020-2021 by adopting new Co-Insurance Model on 80:20 proportionate sharing basis 
and permitted the Director of Agriculture to call for a short tender for se!ection of Insurance Companies. According 
to this model, the Government will act as Co-Insurer with the Implementing agencies on 80:20 proportionate 
sharing basis. Further, in the Government letter dated 13.10.2020,. the Government have directed that the State 
will share Administrative Cost, subject to condition that the State Government’s share shall be only 80% of the 
Administrative Cost which includes Bank Service Charges, Intermediary/Business Procurement charges, Publicity 
Expenses and Claim Management Expenses subject to an overall ceiling of 1.6% of the Gross Premium receivable 
by the Insurance Company.
4. 
In Government Order third read above , the Government have approved the Cluster-wise Implementing 
Agencies selected by the Tender ﬁ nalising Committee and also approved the district-wise, crop-wise Actuarial 
Premium Rates quoted by the selected Insurance Companies and permitted the Director of Agriculture to issue 
work orders to IFFCO TOKIO for Clusters I, !I and Ill and Agriculture Insurance Company of India Limited for 
Clusters IV, V and. VI.
5. 
In that Government Order, the Government have accorded administrative sanction for a sum of 
Rs.1470.7124 Crore (Agriculture : Rs.1436.328 Crore ; Horticulture : Rs.34.3844 Crore) towards the settlement 
of state share of premium subsidy . The Government have also directed the Director of Agriculture to carry out 
Crop Cutting Experiments very carefully and correctly avoiding any moral hazards besides closely monitoring the 
implementation to ensure accuracy and reliability and to verify the area coverage by farmers carefully, particularly 
in the tail end districts thereby minimising claims for early seasqn crop losses.
6. 
In continuation, in Government Order fourth read above, the Government have ordered for notiﬁ cation 
of the Crops, Districts, Firkas, Revenue Villages, Insurance Units and Approved Premium Rates for Agricultural and 
Horticultural Crops for the eﬀ ective implementation of Revamped Pradhan • Mantri Fasal Sima Yojana (RPMFBY) 
during Special Season 2020-2021.
7. 
It is submitted that the 48ih State Level Coordination Committee on Crop Insurance meeting for 
notifying Special season 2020-2021 was held on 12.11.2020 under the Chairmanship of Agricultural Production 
Commissioner and Principal Secretary to Government, Agriculture Department. During the above meeting, State 
Level Co-ordination Committee on Crop Insurance (SLCCCI) has given approval for the agenda points as detailed 
in the minutes of the 481h State Level Co-ordination Committee on Crop Insurance (SLCCCI) meeting.
8. 
The Director of Agriculture in his letter sixth read above has sent a detailed proposal for notiﬁ cation of 
crops, districts, insurance Units (ﬁ rkas, revenue villages), Insurance Companies, Sum Insured and approved premium 
rates for Agriculture and Horticulture Crops during Rabi season, 2020-2021 under Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal 
Sima Yojana (RPMFBY) and requested the Government to issue orders on the recommendation made by 48!h 
State L.eyel .Co-ordination Committee on Crop Insurance (SLCCCI) and also to publish the notiﬁ cation in the 
Government Gazette. The D!rector of Agriculture has also requested to issue “Go ahead” order for implementation 
of Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal Sima Yojana during Rabi season 2020-2021, similar to that of Special season 
as the cut-oﬀ  date for some of the district-crop combinations falls on 30.11.2020. 
9. 
Accordingly, in the D.O letter seventh read above, the Government have issued ‘Go ahead’ order 
to the Director of Agriculture for notiﬁ cation of crops and Insurance Units, Sum Insured and approved premium 
rates for Agricultural and Horticultural crops for Implementation of Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana 
(RPMFBY) during Rabi Season 2020 - 2021, since notiﬁ cation of critical level cut oﬀ  date for enrolment of certain 
crops for some of the districts is 30th November 2020 and the farmers do not suﬀ er due to last minute rush.
10. The Government after careful examination of the proposal of the Director of Agriculture and issue the 
following orders:-
(i) 
to implement Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana by adopting the New Co-Insurance model 
on 80:20 proportionate sharing basis as per approved premium rate for Rabi season, 2020-2021 with 
the following tenderers and contract agreement signed between the Director of Agriculture and the 


3
Insurance companies as per the instructions of the Government of India vide letter No.13012/06/2020
Credit-11 (FTS 8398), dated 19.5.2020 subject to the conditions mentioned in their letters dated 
30.09.2020 and 13.10.2020 and as per the orders issued G.O.(D).No.194, Agriculture (AP2) Department, 
dated 28.10.2020. Details are given in Annexure-I.
(ii) 
to notify the crops in the DistriCts and the Insurance units (Revenue villages for major crops & 
Firkas for other crops) during Rabi Season 2020-2021 as per Annexure II.
(iii) 
to adopt the cluster-wise, district-wise sum insured, approved premium rate and the premium rate for 
farmers for crops to be notiﬁ ed during Rabi season 2020- 2021 as per Annexure-Ill.
(iv) 
to adopt the farmer’s share of premium of 1.5% of sum insured for all Cereals, Millets, Pulses and 
Oilseeds crops or actuarial rate whichever is less and 5% of sum insured for annual Commercial 
crops, annual/perennial horticulture (Operational Guidelines of Revamped PMFBY (Para 13.1))
(v) 
to notify the cluster-wise, district-wise crop-wise seasonality discipline as per Annexure IV.
(vi) 
to notify the district-wise, crop-wise seasonality discipline for furnishing Crop Cutting Experiments (CCE) 
Form I & II and actual yield data by the Department of Economics and Statistics as per Annexure 
V and permit to adopt Para 16.5 of Operational Guidelines of RPMFBY.
(vii) 
to notify the district-wise crop-wise list for notiﬁ cation of perils such as prevented/failed sowing, mid-
season adversities, post harvest losses and localized calamities as per Annexure VI.
(viii) to permit to treat the next working day as the cut-oﬀ  date in case if the cut-oﬀ  date falls on a publi 
holiday or is declared as public holiday by the Government or there is disruption of services due to 
strikes/shut-down etc as per the Para 16.5 of Operational Guidelines of RPMFBY.
(ix) 
to permit the Director of Agriculture and Director of Horticulture & Plantation Crops to sort out any 
discrepancies pointed out by the Implementing Agencies before Gazette Notiﬁ cation. 
11. The Government permit
a. the Department of Economics & Statistics to provide the normal area sovitn details within 15 
days of the notiﬁ cation for the season.
b. the Insurance Companies to reconcile with the Department of Economics & Statistics (DES) for 
any discrepancies noted in the hierarchy of the notiﬁ ed units at any stage of implementation of 
the scheme during enrolment, implementation/uploading in Government portal while processing 
the compensation .
c. 
the Director of Agriculture/Director of Horticulture and Plantation Crops to address the Government 
of India seeking concurrence for uti!isirig MNCFC data wherever area discrepancy is more than 
30% and to provide monthly forecast report on sowing.
12. The Government permit to adopt
a. the existing procedure for conduct of CCEs till standardised protocol is devised by Government 
of India for Smart Sampling Technique (SST) and Two-Step Process of Loss Estimation.
b. Priority of applicability of yield estimate in instances where required number of CCEs could 
not be conducted due to non-availability of adequate cropped area, adverse weather conditions/
inadequate infrastructure etc. as stated below:
o 
Priority 1: Adopting the yield of neighbouring Insurance Unit with maximum correlation
o 
Priority 2: Adopting yield estimate of next higher unit
c. 
State Level Technical Advisory Committee (STAG) to decide ﬁ nally in case of any disputes regarding 
adoption of yield of a neighbouring Insurance Unit.
13. The Government also direct the Insurance Companies to -
a. adopt the Variable threshold Yield for claim calculation
b. calculate and upload the threshold yield at Insurance unit level in the National Crop Insurance 
Portal.


4
c. 
furnish the threshold yield to Government after receipt of CCE Yield data from Department of 
Economics and Statistics.
d. to make an appeal to invoke prevented sowing/germination risk oniy within prescribed period 
with proper evidence (i.e.) not later than 15 days from cut oﬀ  date for enrolment of farmer for 
prevented sowing (Para 21.3.5.11 of OGs) and not later than 3 weeks after peak sowing period 
in case of germination risk (G.O.(Ms).No.293, Agriculture (AP6) Department dated 12.09.2016). 
The Insurance Companies shall also not make an appeal at any date later than the timeline 
ﬁ xed or after the receipt of yield data from the Department of Economics & Statistics.
e. to escalate the same in writing to State Government within the stipulated timeline in case the 
provision is not invoked by the District Level Monitoring Committee/District Level Joint Committee. 
(Para 21.3.5.11. of OGs). ‘
14. The Government instruct-
a. the Insurance Companies to sign necessary Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with TNAU 
for use of Remote Sensing Technology.
b. the TNAU to provioe Remote sensing based information and Crop Signatures on payment within 
one month from the date of receipt of request by the selected Insurance Companies.
c. 
the TNAU to abide by G.O.(Ms) No.293, Agriculture (AP-9) Department dated 12.09.2016 with 
regard to prevented I failed sowing risks.
d. the TNAU to add new crops s..ubsequently and obtain approval from SLCCCI in due course.
e. TNAU to assess yield for Paddy & Maize on pilot basis in any of the districts and submit .the 
yield estimates along with the ground truthing report for approval by SLCCCI.
15. The Government authorize the District Level Monitoring Committee (DLMC) under the Chairmanship of 
District Collector to ﬁ x the seasonality discipline for prevented sowing/failed sowing not later than 15 days from 
cut-oﬀ  date for enrolment by adopting the conditions formulated in the Revamped Operational Guidelines.
16. The Government also direct the Director of Agriculture/Director of Horticulture and Plantation Crops to 
follow the other instructions issued in the Government Order fourth read above scrupulously for the implementation 
of Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana during Rabi season 2020-21.
17. The Government further direct that the claims under the Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana 
Scheme shall be settled on the basis of yield data furnished by the Commissioner, Department of Economics and 
Statistics as per prescribed cut-oﬀ  dates for the notiﬁ ed crops/districts/ﬁ rkas/revenue villages as per Annexure V 
for the eﬀ ective implementation of Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana scheme for Rabi season 2020-21 
and not on the basis of the Annavari Certiﬁ cate/Gazette notiﬁ cation declaring the area as Drought/Flood/Cyclone 
aﬀ ected etc., by the District Collectors.
ANNEXURE I
(G.O.(Ms) No.276, Agriculture (AP2) Department, dated 27.11.2020.)
REVAMPED PRADHAN MANTRI FASAL BIMA YOJANA (RPMFBY) NOTIFICATION FOR
RABI SEASON 2020-2021
Revamped Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (RPMFBY) will be implemented in all the districts 
of Tamil Nadu. (except Chennai) during Rabi season as per the Revamped Operational Guidelines of 
Government of India (Government of India Letter No. 13015/02/2015- Credit - II dated 24.03.2020 & Letter 
No.13012/06/2020 Credit- II (FTS 8398), dated 19.5.2020.
I.  OBJECTIVES OF RPMFBY:.
RPMFBY aims at supporting sustainable production in agriculture by way of
a) Providing ﬁ nancial support to farmers suﬀ ering crop loss/damage arising out of unforeseen events.


5
b) Stabilizing the income of farmers to ensure their continuance in farming.
c) Encouraging farmers to adopt innovative and modern Agriculture practices.
d) Ensuring credit worthiness of the farmers and their continuance in the farming activities, crop diversiﬁ cation 
and enhancing growth and competitiveness of agriculture sector besides protecting farmers from production 
risks.
II.  COVERAGE OF FARMERS:
All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing the notiﬁ ed crops in the notiﬁ ed areas are 
eligible for coverage. However, farmers should have insurable interest for the insured crops and lands. Scheme 
is optional for all loanee and non-loanee farmers.
Ill.  COVERAGE OF RISKS AND EXCLUSIONS:
 
1.  BASIC COVER:
The basic cover under the scheme covers the risk of loss of yield to standing crop (sowing to harvesting). 
This comprehensive risk insurance is provided to cover yield losses on an area based approach basis due to 
non preventable risks like drought, dry spells, ﬂ ood, inundation, wide spread pest and disease attack, landslides, 
natural ﬁ re due to lightening, storm, hailstorm, and cyclone .
 
2.  ADD-ON COVERAGE:
a. PREVENTED SOWING/PLANTING/GERMINATION RISK- If Insured area is prevented from sowing I 
planting I germination due to deﬁ cit rainfall or adverse seasonal/ climatic conditions .
b.  MID-SEASON ADVERSITY - Insurance cover will be provided to the farmers to provide immediate 
relief in case of adverse seasonal conditions viz. ﬂ oods, prolonged dry spells, severe drought etc., 
during the crop season wherein expected yield during the season is likely to be less than 50% of 
Normal Yield.
c.  POST-HARVEST LOSSES - Coverage is available only upto a maximum period of two weeks from 
harvesting, for those crops which are required to be dried in cut and spread/small bundled condition 
in the ﬁ eld after harvesting against speciﬁ c perils of hailstorm, cyclone, cyclonic rains and unseasonal 
rains.
d.  LOCALIZED CALAMITIES - Loss I . damage to notiﬁ ed insured crops resulting from occurrence of 
identiﬁ ed localized risks of hailstorm, landslide, inundation (except Paddy, Sugarcane), cloudburst and 
natural ﬁ re due to lightening aﬀ ecting isolated farms in the notiﬁ ed area.
e.  GENERAL EXCLUSIONS - Losses arising out of war and nuclear risks, malicious damage and other 
preventable risks shall be excluded.
IV.  NOTIFICATION OF IMPLEMENTING AGENCY
RPMFBY shall be implemented for Rabi season 2020-2021 with the following Insurance Companies as per 
the tender agreement in all the districts except Chennai
Clusters
Approved Implementing Agency
I, II & Ill
IFFCO TOKIO General Insurance Company Limited
IV,V& VI
Agriculture Insurance Company of India Limited.
V.   NOTIFICATION OF CLUSTER & INDEMNITY LEVEL
The Indemnity Level for the purpose of calculation of Threshold yield is ﬁ xed at 90% for low risk districts 
, 80% for medium risk districts and 70% for high risk districts. The following six clusters and also the Indemnity 
Level indicated thereon are notiﬁ ed for the purpose of calculation of Threshold yield.

VII. FORM OF FINAL PRODUCE TO BE ADOPTED FOR COMPUTATION OF COMPENSATION CLAIMS:

VIII.   MANDATORY DOCUMENTS FOR ENROLMENT
The following documents are mandatory for enrolment
• 
Proposal form
• 
Registration form
• 
Adangal
o 
Adangal issued by VAO alone for enrolment of Loai1ee & Non-Loanee farmers in normal 
cases.
o 
Sowing certiﬁ cate issued by VAO in case of prevented sowing andadvance purchase of 
insurance before sowing
• 
A copy of Aadhar Card
• 
A copy of ﬁ rst page of Bank Pass Book

1.     Add-On Cover 1: Preventive/failed Sowing and Preventive Planting/Germination (PSPPG) 
2.     Add-On Cover 2: Mid-Season Adversity (MSA)
3.     Add-On Cover 3: Post Harvest Losses (PHL) along with the speciﬁ c list of perils attached separately 
4.     Add-On Cover 4: Localized Risks (LR) ; IN - Inundation; CB - Cloud Burst ; NF - Natural Fire
